name: Marbled Chocolate Cheesecake (Lo Cal)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over medium-high heat, bring 2/3 cup milk to a boil and set aside.
2. In a blender container, sprinkle gelatin over the remaining 1/4 cup cold skim milk; let stand for 4 to 5 minutes.
3. Add the boiling skim milk to the blender; cover and blend at low speed for 2 minutes.
4. Add 6 tbsp sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla extract; blend at medium speed until well mixed.
5. Add low fat cottage cheese; blend at high speed until smooth.
6. Remove 3/4 cup of this mixture to use for marbling and chill until the mixture begins to thicken.
7. In a separate bowl, combine 1 tbsp sugar and cocoa powder; add to the blender container and blend until mixed.
8. Pour the chocolate mixture into a large bowl and chill, stirring occasionally, until it mounds when dropped from a spoon.
9. In a small bowl with an electric mixer at high speed, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 6 tbsp sugar; beat until stiff peaks form.
10. Fold the beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture using a spatula.
11. Pour the chocolate mixture into the prepared crust.
12. Using a knife or spatula, gently swirl the vanilla mixture over the chocolate to create a marbled effect.
13. Cover and refrigerate until firm, about 4 to 5 hours.
14. To make the chocolate crumb crust, combine crumbs and cocoa powder in a bowl; add melted butter and stir until evenly blended.
15. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
